introText: If you work in Modesto and have been wrongfully terminated, discriminated against, harassed, or denied fair wages, The Azizi Firm is here to help. As a California employment law firm, we represent workers throughout Stanislaus County against employers of all sizes - from agricultural processing companies and food manufacturers to healthcare providers and logistics operations.
localInfo: Modesto is the economic center of Stanislaus County, with major employers in agriculture, food processing, healthcare, and manufacturing. The city is at the heart of California's agricultural Central Valley. The California DLSE Modesto field office and the EEOC Fresno Local Office handle employment claims from Stanislaus County workers. The Stanislaus County Superior Court in downtown Modesto hears employment law cases including wrongful termination, discrimination, harassment, and wage disputes.

sections.header: Employment Law Cases We Handle in Modesto
sections.body: Our Modesto employment attorneys handle: wrongful termination, workplace discrimination (race, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation), sexual harassment, hostile work environment, whistleblower retaliation, wage and hour violations (unpaid wages, overtime, meal/rest breaks), employee misclassification, FMLA/CFRA violations, and employment contract disputes.

sections.header: California Employment Laws That Protect Modesto Workers
sections.body: The California Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) provides stronger protections than federal law. California Labor Code sections 1102.5, 98.6, and 6310 protect whistleblowers. The California Family Rights Act (CFRA) provides job-protected leave. Agricultural and food processing workers have specific protections under California wage orders and safety regulations.

faq.question: How long do I have to file an employment claim in California?
faq.answer: Statute of limitations vary by claim type. For FEHA discrimination claims, you must first file with the California Civil Rights Department (CRD) within three years. For wrongful termination claims, you generally have two years. For wage claims, you generally have three years. Contact us immediately to ensure your claim is timely filed.

faq.question: What compensation can I recover in an employment law case?
faq.answer: You may be entitled to lost wages (back pay and front pay), emotional distress damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, reinstatement, and other remedies. The value depends on your specific situation - call us for a free evaluation.

whyHireText: We understand California employment law and have experience litigating against large employers. Our firm's founder previously defended major insurance companies, giving us insider knowledge of how defendants approach employment cases. We leverage this experience to fight for maximum compensation for our clients.
contactUsText: If you're facing a workplace issue in Modesto, contact The Azizi Firm today for a free, confidential consultation. We serve employees throughout Stanislaus County and all of California.
